title: Unity content bundle resource pipeline for mobile games
summary: Production Unity mobile games rely on an content bundle-based resource pipeline that separates the slim native binary from remotely delivered assets. The pipeline covers bundle splitting strategies, dependency manifest management, CDN-based distribution, delta hot-update flows, version integrity verification, and graceful fallback. These patterns apply equally to Godot .pck or Web fetch-based asset loading as general mobile game resource delivery principles.
designRules: Keep the base Android install package/IPA as small as possible; ship only critical launch assets locally; download everything else on first launch or on-demand.
Split content bundles by logical axis: per-scene bundles, per-feature bundles, shared-library bundles, and high-churn bundles (config tables, runtime script scripts, UI templates).
Every content bundle build must produce a catalog manifest listing bundle name, version, dependencies, file size, and integrity hash.
Remote resource index (version manifest) lives on CDN and is fetched first on launch; it declares the latest version, per-bundle metadata, and CDN base URL.

Hot-update flow: fetch remote version manifest → compare with local manifest → compute delta (added, modified, deleted bundles) → download delta bundles with retry and resume → verify integrity per bundle → atomically swap local catalog → apply changes on next launch or live-reload.
Integrity verification uses content hashing (MD5, SHA256, or CRC32) on each downloaded bundle before accepting it; reject and retry on mismatch.
Graceful fallback: if CDN is unreachable or version check fails, continue with currently cached local bundles; never block the player from entering the game.

Asset classification: UI engineTemplates/atlases, 3D models/textures/materials, audio clips, scene bundles, configuration tables (ScriptableObject/JSON/runtime script tables), and runtime script script bundles each belong to dedicated content bundle groups with independent update cadences.

assetClassification.ui: UI engineTemplates, sprite atlases, fonts. Updated frequently with UI changes. Packed per-feature or per-panel group.
assetClassification.models: 3D meshes, textures, materials, animations, avatar parts. Large size, moderate update frequency. Packed per-character or per-scene.
assetClassification.audio: BGM, SFX, voice-over clips. Streamed or preloaded. Grouped by audio bank with compression level control.
assetClassification.scenes: Unity scene files and their exclusive dependencies. Each scene and its unique assets form one bundle; shared assets go to shared bundles.
assetClassification.config: Game balance tables, localization strings, feature flags. Small size, very high update frequency. Packed as a single small bundle for fast delta sync.
assetClassification.runtime-script: runtime script source or bytecode scripts. High update frequency. Packed as a single bundle or split by module for large codebases.

versioningStrategy.scheme: Semantic versioning for the overall resource catalog; per-bundle content hashing for deduplication and delta detection.
versioningStrategy.manifest: Remote catalog JSON declares: version string, min-app-version, per-bundle (name, hash, size, dependencies, CDN relative path, compression).
versioningStrategy.delta: Client compares local hash against remote hash per bundle; downloads only bundles whose hash differs. Supports byte-level patching for large bundles when network is constrained.
versioningStrategy.rollback: Keep the previous successful catalog snapshot; if the new version fails to load (crash count threshold or integrity check failure), automatically revert to the previous snapshot on next launch.

mgaiUsage: When generating Unity, Godot, or Web game project scaffolds, produce a RESOURCE_PIPELINE.md describing the bundle splitting strategy, manifest schema, version check flow, integrity verification method, CDN layout, and fallback policy. Also generate a resource-classification table mapping asset types to bundle groups.
sourcePolicy: Public-safe generalized resource pipeline pattern derived from community knowledge of Unity content bundle workflows. No proprietary bundle names, CDN endpoints, decompiled code, or app-specific metadata.
